channel.title: Comments for page "Enjoy the Silence"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Enjoy the Silence"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You guys should keep going with the CM you have honestly. The personal version of him in my head is pretty much mutually exclusive with yours. (As in far enough apart to fulfill the trope of good twin/Evil twin)

That said, if you're still curious just for the hell of it, feel free to PM.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Chrysalises. You definitely mean Chrysalises. The only thing raining chrysalids evokes in me is terror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey Myriad, glad you enjoyed it - as soon as we decided on a sonic-related MTF for the contest, we know that Containment Specialist Zoe Smith was a perfect candidate for an ex-team member, so we really wanted to use your skip.

CM will probably be turning up again in the wider On Mount Golgotha canon, so feel free to PM any thoughts on his character. I believe firmly in contacting authors beyond the grave :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was fun to read and quite well written. I have to admit, I'm more than a little delighted that you chose to tie in my old article.

CM himself is a bit different from how I imagined him - but then again, I believe quite firmly in 'Death of the Author'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the image of chrysalids raining on the open grave is both strange and strangely moving. It's my favourite moment from our entries, and (in relation also to Kalinin's comment below) I think the whole confrontation here reinforces that Eta-11 doesn't generally face physical challenges, but emotional ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's strange to have something that sets the mood that this does while maintaining action. It could be that outside of the context of its companion entries, this piece would be dissonant for that, but this was interesting and different enough that it gets my support.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really great. 1638 is a good skip to include in this canon, and the fact that you had it fight 2402 is just awesome. XD Plus, I love what's-his-name's narration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn, I was already loving this, but then you tell me this is all leading to more Mount Golgotha stuff? I can't wait!

And CumaeanSibyl, psul is right: you should be terrifically proud of yourself, this is great stuff - both the idea and the writing. The tie-in to 1638 is incredibly clever, not to mention seamless, and you actually managed to make me care even more about the already-powerful Zoe Smith narrative.

Well done, really. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do prefer the other tales in this series over this to be honest, but this is worth my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beautiful piece. Sharing a name with one of my all time favorite songs is icing on the cake. ^_^
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also want to say that I'm really proud to have CumaeanSibyl on my team for this. My first submission was also for a contest, and I have been following CS's honest and useful feedback on the forums for a while. A wonderful team member, and the result is terrific!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tale has a link to SCP-1638 that explains a lot, especially who is involved, who is buried, how Zoe died, everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked the other tales but this just ruins it…
Format, Bleh.
Doesn't explain why he is there, what they are doing, or anything. Just talks about their actions and who is walking where. Are they burying someone? Who died and how?
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the idea behind this Tale is one of the strongest in the competition, and I'd like to take a second to explain why. Sorry, I know it's my own team, but I think some behind-the-scenes information is important.

It would be very easy to imagine that this was always planned as the conclusion to the Eta-11 arc, and that the steps to get here were filled in backwards. In fact, early drafts of both the AWCY? proposal and SCP-2402 were already written, and we had been kicking around the idea that Zoe Smith (from Myriad's wonderful SCP-1638) would have been a member of Eta-11.

CumaeanSibyl came up with an idea that tied in the nightmare music from A Convincing Lyre and Dreams of Failure, the characters from Dreams of Failure and SCP-1638 (as well as the MTF itself), the anomaly from SCP-2402 (bearing in mind that the final note for that skip hadn't been written when this tale came into being), and which perfectly expressed the themes and the emotions which Eta-11 addresses - fear of worthlessness, aging into obsolescence, regrets, the bonds of family and loving someone even when you know that they are in the wrong.

Pulling all of those strands together into a single idea would have been difficult enough if it was planned out at the start. To do it mid-contest was, I think, tremendous. And I think the execution is worthy of the idea - CS, your first site submission is one to be really proud of.

Oh yeah, and did I mention that there is some stuff in here that is going to seriously pay off in the On Mount Golgotha canon? Stay tuned!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A neat conclusion for this arc. The mediation on silence, and absent comrades in the midst of what is basically the final action sequence of a thriller is a nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Entry for the 2016 MTF Contest, Team "Site-19 String Quartet." Thanks to psul, WrongJohnSilver, and Smapti for their help, for their patience, and for inviting me to play in their sandbox.

Suggested reading order:

  • A Convincing Lyre
  • An Impenitent Thief
  • Project Proposal 2014-2112: "Dreams of Failure"
  • SCP-2402
  • Enjoy the Silence
  • The Musical Suspects
